routes.title: Machine-check the 2025 transposition reduction
routes.statement: Formalize the published reduction with explicit packing, simulation, size map, and time overhead.
routes.mechanism: Replay every reduction step in a proof assistant while preserving the exact multitape model and asymptotic overhead.

routes.title: Exact query lower bound from a common sensitivity witness
routes.statement: Exact multiplication requires 2n bit queries in the worst case in the deterministic adaptive bit-query model.
routes.mechanism: Use x=y=2^n-1 as a single input at which every one of the 2n input bits is sensitive.

routes.title: Multiscale communication cuts for binary transposition
routes.statement: Each logarithmic spatial scale forces Omega(m^2) aggregate information crossing, yielding Omega(m^2 log m).
routes.mechanism: Partition tape positions at nested scales and charge matrix bits whose source and destination lie on opposite sides of each cut.
assumptions: head movement and rewriting cannot bypass charged cuts
falsification_tests: construct a packing schedule that crosses several scales in one move
test whether one crossing can discharge multiple charges

routes.title: Crossing-sequence bound for oblivious multiplication
routes.statement: Oblivious bounded-tape multiplication requires superlinear information transport across tape cuts.
routes.mechanism: Associate crossing sequences with information that must move between input blocks and output blocks under an oblivious head schedule.
proof_obligations: input family with independent cross-cut influence

routes.title: Formal barrier theorem for bare information counting
routes.statement: Bare input/output cardinality arguments cannot by themselves yield Omega(n log n) time in a model permitting reversible computation.
routes.mechanism: Construct a family of computations sharing the same information cardinality but having different time costs, exposing the missing transport premise.
falsification_tests: attempt to derive a time bound from the declared argument class

routes.title: Landauer erasure proves optimal multiplication time
routes.statement: Thermodynamic erasure cost proves every multiplication algorithm takes Omega(n log n) Turing steps.
routes.mechanism: Convert an asserted number of erased bits into a time lower bound.

routes.title: Benchmark crossover proves asymptotic optimality
routes.statement: No measured implementation beats n log n, therefore no asymptotically faster algorithm exists.
routes.mechanism: Extrapolate finite performance curves to all algorithms and input sizes.
falsification_tests: introduce an algorithm with a large asymptotic crossover

routes.title: Transfer the online lower bound directly to offline multiplication
routes.statement: The known online Omega(n log n) result proves the unrestricted offline lower bound.
routes.mechanism: Treat the online theorem as if it applied to the larger offline algorithm class.
falsification_tests: exhibit an offline algorithm that violates online access restrictions

routes.title: Transfer a bounded-circuit lower bound without simulation theorem
routes.statement: A bounded-coefficient circuit lower bound automatically proves the multitape Turing lower bound.
routes.mechanism: Identify two different computational models without an explicit reduction.
falsification_tests: compare operations and access patterns of the two models
